proposal_text: This research proposes to analyse anonymised Dublin Bus and Luas smart card tap-on data to model public transport usage patterns across different times of day, routes, and weather conditions. The dataset consists of aggregated journey counts per route per hour provided by the National Transport Authority under an open data licence. No individual passenger records are included and no re-identification is possible from the aggregated data. The analysis will use time series forecasting and clustering algorithms to identify underserved routes and peak demand periods. Results will be shared with Dublin City Council to inform transport planning decisions. The environmental impact of the computational resources required for the analysis will be documented. No AI system will be deployed in any operational context.

assessment.risk_summary: This research proposal involves the analysis of anonymised public transport data, which poses risks to privacy and potentially infringes on environmental protection rights. The use of time series forecasting and clustering algorithms may also perpetuate biases if not carefully considered.

identified_risks.risk: Perpetuation of biases in data and biased code/model outputs
identified_risks.explanation: The use of time series forecasting and clustering algorithms may perpetuate existing biases if not carefully considered.
The computational resources required for the analysis may have an environmental impact, which needs to be documented and mitigated.

charter_rights_at_risk.relevance: The research may have an environmental impact due to the use of computational resources
The research aims to inform transport planning decisions, which is a good administrative purpose

historical_precedents.lesson: Bias in data and algorithms can have serious consequences for individuals and society
Algorithmic decision-making can perpetuate existing biases if not carefully considered

recommended_mitigations.mitigation: Use bias-aware algorithms and techniques to mitigate potential biases in data and code/model outputs
Document and mitigate the environmental impact of computational resources

tier1_mandatory_actions: Conduct a comprehensive evaluation of the research approach and its impacts
tier2_reflective_prompts: Consider whether the research could be used to perpetuate existing biases or infringe on individual rights
